---
name: striatum-supervise
description: Run a long-lived agent session that spans multiple Striatum work packets. Load when the operator asks to "supervise" an agent or when MCP/PTY bootstrap context is present.
version: "2.10.0"

profile: claude_code

# Striatum: Supervised Sessions

RFC 0009/RFC 0050 can hold a single agent process alive across multiple work
packets. The current path is daemon MCP: the supervisor exports the endpoint,
token, repository, and session context, then the agent calls `tools/list` and
`work.await_packet`. The older stdin packet delivery path remains a CLI
compatibility route for wrappers that still consume `supervise send`.

## When to invoke

- The lane has `harness_profile.supervision.compatible: true` and the
  operator wants the agent process held across packets.
- The operator mentioned MCP bootstrap, agent-loop, `supervise start`, or
  `supervise send`.

## Authoritative reference

- `striatum supervise start` — Start a long-lived agent CLI for the session.
- `striatum supervise send` — Deliver a packet to a supervisor's stdin pipe.
- `striatum supervise stop` — Stop a supervisor (SIGTERM, SIGKILL after 5s).
- `striatum supervise status` — Probe a supervisor's liveness.
- `striatum supervise list` — List supervisors for a run.

Use daemon MCP for packet work. `striatum <verb> --help` documents the CLI
compatibility fallback for supervisor lifecycle operations.

## Common patterns

```json
{"method":"tools/list","params":{"repository_id":"<repository-id>"}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"session.register","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"run_id":"<run-id>","role":"<role>","lane":"<lane>","fresh":true}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"session.report","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"session_id":"<session-id>","report_kind":"ready","phase":"discovery","message":"MCP tools discovered"}}}
{"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"work.await_packet","repository_id":"<repository-id>","arguments":{"session_id":"<session-id>","lease_seconds":1800}}}
```

Stdout and stderr may be sent to `DEVNULL` or a tmux pane for inspection; the
runner does not parse supervisor output for state.

If you are operating a legacy stdin wrapper, use `data.packet_id` from the
claim result; `message_id`, `lease_id`, `job_id`, `session_id`, and
`supervisor_id` are different identifiers and are not accepted as
`--packet-id`.

## Where to look next

- **`striatum-claim-loop`** — the per-packet verb sequence.
- **`striatum-recover`** — when a supervisor is `lost`.

## What not to do

- Do not bypass the daemon (the CLI is the only client; the daemon is the single writer per D094 / RFC 0043); never open Postgres directly, and do not treat `.striatum/` scratch files as workflow state.
- Do not treat marker files, tmux panes, or terminal output as workflow state.
- Do not advance state by printing phrases; advance by calling the CLI verbs in your work packet.
- Do not capture stdout/stderr transcripts; D028 keeps them off by default.
- Do not derive bylines from job titles; use the byline supplied in your work packet.
- Do not parse a supervisor's own output for workflow state; supervisors send DEVNULL.
